info.title: Reality Analysis
info.description:
Interact with your data analysis jobs.

Create, update, delete jobs.

get.description:
Retrieve job progress.

### Remarks

The job progression is deemed valid when the job is active and won't be available after 30 days.

User should query with backoff intervals of 15, 30, 60, 120 seconds. The interval between the first and second query is 15 seconds and then 30 seconds. If the percentage changes the sequence is restarted.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: Header Authorization was not found in the request. Access denied.

404.description: This response indicates that the specified job progress was not found.

error.message: Requested job progress is not available.

429.description: This response indicates that the client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

retry-after.description: Number of seconds to wait until client is allowed to make more requests.

error.message: The client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

patch.description:
---

Patch an existing detector

Patch can be used for changing the display name, description, or documentation URL of a detector. Any combination of these fields can be provided.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

---

get.description:
---

Retrieve all available detectors.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

---

parameters.name: $filter
parameters.description:
The $filter query option requests a specific set of detectors.
The given filter is evaluated for each detector and only detectors where the filter evaluates to true are returned.
Properties supported for filtering: `labels`, `exports`.
Supported operators: `and`, `or`, `not`, `in`.
Examples of $filter values:
`$filter=exports in ('Polygons', 'Lines') and labels in ('crack')`
`$filter=(not exports in ('Locations')) and labels in ('traffic sign')`
`$filter=not (exports in ('Lines') or labels in ('crack'))`

200.description: This response indicates that the request was successful.

detectors.name: @bentley/cracks-ortho
detectors.description: Detects cracks in concrete infrastructure to enable defect inspection workflows.

detectors.name: traffic-signs
detectors.description: Detects all traffic signs within a scene.

400.description: The 400 (Bad Request) status code indicates that the request cannot be processed by the server due to a client error (e.g. malformed request syntax).

error.message: Cannot retrieve detectors.

details.message: $filter contains an invalid or unsupported statement.

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: Header Authorization was not found in the request. Access denied.

404.description: This response indicates that the requested operation or resource could not be found.

error.message: The requested resource was not found. Verify the API URL and the Accept header.

details.message: Unable to match incoming request to an operation.

retry-after.description: Number of seconds to wait until client is allowed to make more requests.

error.message: The client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

get.description:
---

Retrieve list of jobs matching provided filter.

The `iTwinId` in filter is optional. If it is not provided, this will return jobs created by the same user. However, if the iTwinId is provided and the user has access to it, this will return jobs associated with that specific iTwin.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

---

parameters.name: $filter
parameters.description:
The $filter query option requests a specific set of jobs.
The given filter is evaluated for each job and only job where the filter evaluates to true are returned.
Properties supported for filtering: `id`, `iTwinId`, `name`, `email`, `type`, `state`, `createdDateTime`.
Supported operators: `eq`, `lt`, `le`, `gt`, `ge`, `ne`, `and`, `or`.
 Examples of $filter values:
`$filter=iTwinId eq dc720f05-e5f7-448b-ae06-765de46f0af1`
`$filter=id eq 9e2d27cf-b695-47e9-b7f0-0d47dc3b88ad`
`$filter=createdDateTime gt 2024-06-24T13:00:00Z`

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: Parameter that enables continuing to the next page of the previous paged query. This must be passed exactly as it is in the response body's _links.next property.

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: The number of reality analysis jobs to get in each page. Max 1000, but 100 is the default if this parameter is not included.

400.description: The 400 (Bad Request) status code indicates that the request cannot be processed by the server due to a client error (e.g. malformed request syntax).

error.message: Cannot find jobs.

details.message: The $top query parameter value must be a positive integer that does not exceed 1000.

ErrorWarningMessage.title: Error and Warning messages
ErrorWarningMessage.description: Model for error and warning messages

JobCreation.title: Job Creation

specifications.description: List of inputs, outputs and options for the job, must be in sync with type.

JobCreation.description: Model for creating a new job.

errors.description: List of potential errors from the job execution.

warnings.description: List of potential warnings from the job execution.

JobMessage.title: Job Message

code.description: Unique identifier for the error or warning.

title.description: Title of the error or warning.

message.description: Message of the error or warning.

params.description: Parameters to be placed in the message. Can be used for localization effort.

JobMessage.description: Details of an error or a warning message.

deprecated.description: Represents if the file is deprecated. A deprecated file should not be used for long term, it will not be available in the future.

ContainerLinks.title: Container Links
ContainerLinks.description: The link to the bucket container.

JobProgress.title: Job Progress
JobProgress.description: Job progress percentage and job state.

userId.description: Identifier of the user that created the job.

specifications.description: List of inputs, outputs and options for the job, must be in sync with the type.

processingUnits.description: The number of processing units consumed by the job.

Objects2DInputs.title: Objects2D Inputs

model3D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene pointing to a collection of point clouds/meshes to process, or a point cloud, or a mesh.

objects2D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene annotated with embedded 2D objects. This input replaces the `photoObjectDetector` input.

photoObjectDetector.description: Reality data id of photo object detector or photo object detector identifier from the Detector Library.

photos.description: Reality data id of ContextScene pointing to the photos to process.

Objects2DInputs.description: Following are the different inputs for the job.

Objects2DOutputsCreate.title: Objects2D Outputs for Creation
Objects2DOutputsCreate.description: Following are the different output types for job creation.

Objects2DOutputs.title: Objects2D Outputs

locations3DAsGeoJSON.description: Reality data id of 3D objects locations as GeoJSON file, `objects3d` output must be defined.

locations3DAsSHP.description: Reality data id of 3D objects locations as SHP file, `objects3d` output must be defined.

objects2D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene, annotated with embedded 2D objects.

objects3D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene, annotated with embedded 3D objects.

objects3DAs3DTiles.description: Reality data id of 3D objects as 3D Tiles file, `objects3d` output must be defined.

objects3DAsGeoJSON.description: Reality data id of 3D objects as GeoJSON file, `objects3d` output must be defined.

Objects2DOutputs.description: Following are the different output types for the job.

maxDist.description: Maximum distance between photos and 3D objects.

minPhotos.description: Minimum number of 2D objects to generate a 3D object.

useTiePoints.description: Improve detection using tie points in oriented photos.

Segmentation2DInputs.title: Segmentation2D Inputs

model3D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene pointing to a collection of point clouds/meshes to process, or a point cloud, or a mesh.

segmentation2D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene pointing to segmented photos. This input replaces `photoSegmentationDetector` input.

photoSegmentationDetector.description: Either reality data id of photo segmentation detector or photo segmentation detector identifier from the Detectors Library.

photos.description: Reality data id of the ContextScene pointing to the photos to process.

Segmentation2DInputs.description: Following are the different inputs for the job.

Segmentation2DOutputsCreate.title: Segmentation2D Outputs for Creation
Segmentation2DOutputsCreate.description: Following are the different output types for job creation.

computeLineWidth.description: Estimation 3D line width at each vertex.

removeSmallLines.description: Remove 3D lines with total length smaller than this value.

minPhotos.description: Minimum number of 2D detection to generate a 3D detection.

Segmentation2DOutputs.title: Segmentation2D Outputs

segmentation2D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene pointing to segmented photos.

segmentedPhotos.description: Reality data id of segmented photos.

lines3D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene, annotated with embedded 3D lines.

lines3DAs3DTiles.description: Reality data id of 3D lines as 3D Tiles file, `lines3d` output must be defined.

lines3DAsGeoJSON.description: Reality data id of 3D lines as GeoJSON file, `lines3d` output must be defined.

polygons3D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene annotated with embedded 3D polygons.

polygons3DAs3DTiles.description: Reality data id of 3D polygons as 3D Tiles file, `polygons3d` output must be defined.

polygons3DAsGeoJSON.description: Reality data id of 3D polygons as GeoJSON file, `polygons3d` output must be defined.

Segmentation2DOutputs.description: Following are the different output types.

SegmentationOrthophotoInputs.title: SegmentationOrthophoto Inputs

orthophoto.description: Reality data id of ContextScene pointing to orthophotos to process.

orthophotoSegmentationDetector.description: Either reality data id of orthophoto segmentation detector or orthophoto segmentation detector identifier from the Detectors Library.

SegmentationOrthophotoInputs.description: Following are the different inputs for job.

SegmentationOrthophotoOutputsCreate.title: SegmentationOrthophoto Outputs for Creation
SegmentationOrthophotoOutputsCreate.description: Following are the different output types for job creation.

SegmentationOrthophotoOutputs.title: SegmentationOrthophoto Outputs

segmentation2D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene, pointing to segmented orthophotos.

segmentedPhotos.description: Reality data id of segmented orthophotos.

ChangeDetectionInputs.description: Following are the different inputs for the job.

filterThreshold.description: Low threshold to detect spatial changes (hysteresis detection).

meshSamplingResolution.description: Target point cloud resolution when starting from meshes.

minPointsPerChange.description: Minimum number of points in a region to be considered as a change.

threshold.description: High threshold to detect spatial changes (hysteresis detection).

ChangeDetectionOutputs.title: ChangeDetection Outputs

changesInModelA.description: Points in A not in B as OPC.

changesInModelB.description: Points in B not in A as OPC.

locations3DAsGeoJSON.description: Reality data id of 3D objects locations as GeoJSON file.

locations3DAsSHP.description: Reality data id of 3D objects locations as SHP format.

objects3D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene annotated with embedded 3D objects.

ChangeDetectionOutputs.description: Following are the different output types for the job.

computeLineWidth.description: Estimation 3D line width at each vertex.

crs.description: CRS used by POD, LAS, LAZ, PLY and SHP outputs.

keepInputResolution.description: To make segmentation 3D output exact same point input.

removeSmallComponents.description: Remove 3D lines with total length smaller than this value.

Segmentation3DInputs.title: Segmentation3D Inputs

extent.description: Path in the bucket of the clipping polygon to apply.

model3D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene pointing to a collection of point clouds/meshes to process, or a point cloud, or a mesh.

pointCloudSegmentationDetector.description: Either reality data id of point cloud segmentation detector or point cloud segmentation detector identifier from the Detectors Library.

segmentation3D.description: Reality data id of ContextScene pointing to a segmented point cloud, this input replaces `pointCloudSegmentationDetector` and `model3D` inputs.

displayName.description: An optional display name of the detector.

description.description: An optional description of the detector.

documentationUrl.description: An optional URL to the detector's documentation.

All existing versions of the detector.

creatorId.description: User Id of the version creator.

DetectorVersionCreateResponseLinks.title: Detector version creation links
DetectorVersionCreateResponseLinks.description: Hyperlinks for further detector version upload and completion.

latestVersionNumber.description: The latest version of the detector with 'Ready' status, if any.

versions.description: All existing versions of the detector.

downloadUrl.description: URL to download the detector version. It is present only if the version status is 'Ready'.

creatorId.description: User Id of the version creator.

displayName.description: Optional new display name for the detector.

description.description: Optional new description for the detector.

documentationUrl.description: Optional new documentation URL for the detector.

DetectorPatch.description: Patch information to be applied to the detector.

next-page-link.title: Next Page Link
next-page-link.description: URL for getting the next page of data, if applicable.

code.description: One of a server-defined set of error codes.

message.description: A human-readable representation of the error.

details.description: Optional array of more specific errors.

DetailedError.description: Contains error information and an array of more specific errors.

DetailedErrorResponse.title: Detailed Error Response
DetailedErrorResponse.description: Gives details for an error that occurred while handling the request. Note that clients MUST NOT assume that every failed request will produce an object of this schema, or that all of the properties in the response will be non-null, as the error may have prevented this response from being constructed.

code.description: One of a server-defined set of error codes.

message.description: A human-readable representation of the error.

ErrorResponse.title: Error Response
ErrorResponse.description: Gives details for an error that occurred while handling the request. Note that clients MUST NOT assume that every failed request will produce an object of this schema, or that all of the properties in the response will be non-null, as the error may have prevented this response from being constructed.
